data.title: Tesla found partially responsible for a fatal crash in 2019, as per lawyers claiming Autopilot could have prevented it.
data.description: Attorneys representing the family of a woman who died after being hit by a Tesla sedan in 2019 claim that the vehicle's Autopilot system could have prevented the accident. This incident raised concerns over the safety of Tesla's technology and highlighted the importance of implementing more advanced safety measures in autonomous vehicles. The tragic event underscores the need for continuous improvement in self-driving car technologies to prevent similar accidents in the future.

data.title: UNITS® Moving and Portable Storage sponsors Timmy Hill's #56 Toyota at Watkins Glen NASCAR Truck Series race on August 8.
data.description: On August 8, 2025, UNITS® Moving and Portable Storage will have their name featured prominently on Hill Motorsports' #56 Toyota as the primary sponsor for a top race event in Watkins Glen, New York. UNITS®, known for their creative moving and storage services, will be showcased during this high-profile event.

data.title: MicroStrategy beats Q2 revenue estimates at $114.49M, EPS at $32.52/share. Introduced Treasury Preferred Stock.
data.description: MicroStrategy Inc (NASDAQ: MSTR) revealed strong sales performance in the second quarter. Their revenue for this period stood at $114.49 million, surpassing the anticipated $112.96 million as per analysts. The company's adjusted earnings per share for Q2 were reported at $32.52. Michael Saylor, the executive chairman, highlighted the introduction of the world's initial Treasury Preferred Stock during the July IPO of STRC, aimed at offering stability in pricing and generating high-yield to a fresh group of investors. For further details, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Boot Barn (BOOT) exceeded Q1 expectations, with earnings at $1.74/share and revenue at $504.067M. FY26 EPS guidance raised to $5.80-$6.70 and sales guidance to $2.100B-$2.180B. CEO John Hazen optimistic about the company's performance.
data.description: Boot Barn Holdings Inc (NYSE: BOOT) announced robust first-quarter results, surpassing expectations. Earnings stood at $1.74 per share, beating the projected $1.52 per share. The company's quarterly sales were $504.067 million, exceeding the anticipated $492.339 million. Consequently, Boot Barn raised its FY2026 guidance, with GAAP EPS now in the range of $5.80 to $6.70 (up from $5.50-$6.40) and sales expected to reach $2.100 billion-$2.180 billion (previously $2.070 billion-$2.150 billion). CEO John Hazen expressed satisfaction with the strong start to fiscal 2026. The full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: AbbVie's Q2 2025 sales hit $15.42B, surpassing estimates. Adjusted earnings at $2.97, raising FY guidance. Humira sales down.
data.description: Pharmaceutical company AbbVie Inc. (NYSE: ABBV) announced their second-quarter sales for 2025 at $15.42 billion, surpassing the expected consensus of $14.98 billion. The sales saw a 6.6% increase on a reported basis or 6.5% operationally. The company also revealed adjusted earnings of $2.97, marking a 12.1% rise from the previous year but falling short of the estimated $3.23. AbbVie adjusted their fiscal 2025 earnings guidance from $11.67-$11.87 per share to $11.88-$12.08, as opposed to the analyst forecast of $12.24. Management hinted at a probable decrease in net pricing in the latter part of the year, while highlighting a positive volume growth trend for their products. This information was reported through Benzinga.com which also covered President Trump's 60-day ultimatum to US pharmaceutical companies to reduce drug prices.

data.title: Ethereum's 10th anniversary sees crypto treasuries surpass $100B, with rising institutional interest in Ether as a treasury asset.
data.description: In honor of Ethereum's 10th anniversary, there has been a surge in institutional interest in Ether as a potential treasury reserve asset. This has led to a shift in focus on Wall Street towards Ether, moving beyond Bitcoin as the primary digital asset of interest for institutional investors.
